process_name: Rare leptonic decay B0 -> mu+ mu-

evidence: Focused grep for B_d.*mu, B0.*mu, b -> d, C10, pseudoscalar, rare_decay, rare decay, rare leptonic, and dimuon in required implementation/test directories found no B0 -> mu+ mu- observable implementation.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:225 defines the existing b_d input as B_d mixing, not B0 -> mu+ mu-.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903 defines evaluate_bd_mixing for Delta F = 2 B_d mixing.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:646 evaluates B_d mixing from the bridge match; no Delta B = 1 rare leptonic branch is present.
commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "B_d.*mu|B0d.*mu|B\\^0_d.*mu|B\\^0.*mu|B0.*mu|b[[:space:]-]*->[[:space:]-]*d|b[[:space:]]+to[[:space:]]+d|C_10|C10|pseudoscalar|rare_decay|rare decay|rare leptonic|dimuon" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

pdg_values.label: HFLAV2023:BdOverBsMuMu
pdg_values.observable: BR(B0 -> mu+ mu-) / BR(B_s0 -> mu+ mu-)

status_history.reason: Initial PKA draft deposit for B0 -> mu+ mu-.
Writer polish completed for WA batch wa_w4_beauty.

issues: CHK-1: TeX post-2008 prose states CMS Run 2 used 140 fb^-1 at sqrt(s)=13 TeV, but those dataset numerical claims are not represented in the YAML pdg_or_equivalent block with source URL, access date, and sha256 provenance.

status_history.reason: Cycle-2 writer rework added CMS Run 2 dataset provenance for the TeX numerical dataset claim.
Cycle-2 checker re-check passed all CHK-1..CHK-8 items for CA batch ca_w4_beauty_v2.
Opus round 1 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_001_index.md.

status_history.notes: All measured and quoted prediction value claims verified against fetched source pages or local source snapshots.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(B^0\to\mu^+\mu^-)\).
This entry covers the rare leptonic \(b\to d\mu^+\mu^-\) flavor-changing
neutral-current decay of the neutral \(B_d\) meson.  It is the \(b\to d\)
analogue of \(B_s^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\), but is CKM-suppressed and currently enters
the catalog as an upper limit rather than an observed branching fraction.
sections.pdg_prose:
The current PDG live/API listing \texttt{S042.7}, accessed on 2026-05-16 and
stored under source key \texttt{PDG2026:BdMuMu}, gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(B^0\to\mu^+\mu^-) < 1.5\times10^{-10}\quad(90\%\ {\rm CL}).
\]
The PDG input used for this limit is the CMS Run 2 result
(\texttt{CMS2023:BdMuMu}), which also reports
\(<1.9\times10^{-10}\) at \(95\%\) CL.  The local canonical snapshot is
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B006/pdg\_2026\_bdmumu.txt}.  As an
equivalent HFLAV rare-decay ratio constraint, the April 2023 table gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(B^0\to\mu^+\mu^-)/\mathcal{B}(B_s^0\to\mu^+\mu^-) < 8.1\times10^{-2}
  \quad(90\%\ {\rm CL}),
\]
using the LHCb input (\texttt{HFLAV2023:BdOverBsMuMu}).  For scale, the
reduced-uncertainty Standard Model prediction recorded in
\texttt{BobethEtAl2013:BdMuMuSM} is
\[
  \mathcal{B}(B_d\to\mu^+\mu^-)_{\rm SM}=(1.06\pm0.09)\times10^{-10}.
\]
sections.relevance:
This mode is a clean \(\Delta B=1\) leptonic rare decay.  In warped/anarchic
flavor models, flavor-changing \(Z\), KK electroweak gauge, Higgs, radion, or
other scalar exchanges can contribute to axial-vector, scalar, and pseudoscalar
\(b\to d\mu\mu\) Wilson coefficients.  Scalar and pseudoscalar terms are
especially diagnostic because they can compete with the helicity-suppressed
Standard Model amplitude.
sections.post_2008:
Since the arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}), this channel moved from
pre-LHC upper limits to a mature LHC rare-decay search.  ATLAS combined Run 1
and 2015--2016 data to set \(2.1\times10^{-10}\) at \(95\%\) CL
(\texttt{ATLAS2019:BdMuMu}).  LHCb later set \(2.6\times10^{-10}\) at
\(95\%\) CL and constrained the \(B^0/B_s^0\) dimuon branching-ratio ratio
(\texttt{LHCb2022:BdMuMu}).  CMS Run 2, with \(140\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) at
\(\sqrt{s}=13~{\rm TeV}\), now supplies the PDG canonical \(90\%\)-CL limit
(\texttt{CMS2023:BdMuMu}; \texttt{PDG2026:BdMuMu}).  On the theory side,
\(B_{s,d}\to\ell^+\ell^-\) Standard Model predictions now include reduced
perturbative electroweak and QCD uncertainties
(\texttt{BobethEtAl2013:BdMuMuSM}).
sections.validity:
The experimental object is robust as an upper limit on a short-distance
branching fraction, but the catalog interpretation is model-dependent.  A live
constraint needs a consistent \(\Delta B=1\) Hamiltonian, CKM convention,
normalization to \(f_{B_d}\), and treatment of scalar/pseudoscalar operators.
Unlike angular \(b\to d\ell\ell\) modes it does not require a multi-bin
form-factor likelihood.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required catalog greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no \(B^0\to\mu^+\mu^-\) or
\(b\to d\mu\mu\) observable implementation.  Nearby hits such as
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:225} and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903} are \(B_d\) \(\Delta F=2\) mixing
entries, not this rare leptonic \(\Delta B=1\) decay.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{MEDIUM.}  This requires a new \(\Delta B=1\) leptonic rare-decay
observable and Wilson normalization for \(C_{10}^{(\prime)}\),
\(C_S^{(\prime)}\), and \(C_P^{(\prime)}\).  It should not need new lattice
matrix-element machinery beyond decay-constant inputs, RG for a full angular
analysis, or exclusive semileptonic form factors.
sections.key_references:
Process-local raw reference keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{PDG2026:BdMuMu}, \texttt{HFLAV2023:BdOverBsMuMu},
\texttt{CMS2023:BdMuMu}, \texttt{LHCb2022:BdMuMu},
\texttt{ATLAS2019:BdMuMu}, \texttt{BobethEtAl2013:BdMuMuSM}, and
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}.
